items.title: Mother's 11-year fight to secure stateless daughter's identification documents continues
items.summary: Tinara, who is a permanent resident originally from Indonesia, said she has been trying to resolve the status of her 17-year-old adopted daughter, Lailatul Kamalia Abdul Rahim, since birth. Tinara said she had made numerous trips to the National Registration Department and the Welfare Department in an effort to regularise her daughter's status. "Rejected, failed, rejected, failed.

items.title: Van driver remanded three days over death of Year Two pupil in Batu Pahat
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, May 6 — A 60-year-old van driver has been remanded for three days starting today to assist police in the probe into the death of a Year Two pupil from a primary school in Parit Sulong here. Batu Pahat police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani said the remand application for the man was made to assist investigations under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act (APJ) 1987. “The remand order on the suspect was issued by Magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman from today to May 8, after he was brought to the Batu Pahat Magistrate’s Court earlier this morning,” he said when contacted by the media today.

items.title: MHO backs MyKAS card for stateless people to address documentation crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A humanitarian organisation has called on the Home Ministry to extend the MyKAS temporary resident identity card to stateless people as a practical measure to address a long-running documentation crisis that poses risks to both the affected individuals and national security. Malaysian International Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) secretary-general Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim said the proposal is not intended to confer citizenship automatically, but rather to ensure that every stateless individual has a recorded identity that can be tracked by the authorities. He said MHO was encouraged to raise the issue after Kuamut state assemblyman Datuk Masiung Banah publicly supported issuing the MyKAS card to stateless or undocumented people in Sabah.

items.title: Malaysia's general insurance gross written premiums rise to RM24.2bil in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's general insurance industry maintained its growth trajectory last year, albeit a slower pace, after expanding 4.8 per cent compared with nearly seven per cent previously. PIAM said underwriting profit increased to RM1.2 billion, up RM125 million year-on-year, with the overall combined ratio holding steady at about 93 per cent. Motor insurance remained the largest segment of the general insurance market, accounting for 45.2 per cent of total premiums, followed by fire insurance at 20.9 per cent.

items.title: Guidelines boost jobs, tackle underemployment among neurodivergent individuals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Guidelines for Employment Support for Neurodivergent Individuals are expected to drive skill-based career pathways while addressing underemployment among persons with learning disabilities. Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said the guidelines, an initiative by the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso), serve as a strategic framework to strengthen inclusive workforce participation. "Persons with disabilities, including those who are neurodivergent, are actively seeking employment.

items.title: Azam Baki: MACC recommends charges against two in Arm Holdings chip probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 6 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recommended two people involved in the chip patent deal with UK semiconductor giant Arm Holdings to be charged, its outgoing chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said today. “Two people. If they (the Attorney General Chambers) don’t think they should be, then okay, but on our side, we recommended the two be charged,” Azam added.

items.title: Palm oil rally seen continuing on biodiesel demand boost, analyst Mistry says
items.summary: MUMBAI: Malaysian palm oil prices are likely to rise about 12 per cent to 5,200 ringgit (US$1,316) a metric ton by mid-July, as higher energy prices from the US.-Israeli war on Iran boost biodiesel demand and tighten supplies, analyst Dorab Mistry said on Wednesday. Palm oil futures are expected to extend gains to around 5,000 ringgit by June and potentially reach 5,200 ringgit by mid-July on biodiesel demand, said Mistry, the director of Indian consumer goods company Godrej International. "Rising energy prices prompted Indonesia to reinstate its B50 palm biodiesel programme from 1 July 2026," Mistry said.

items.title: Elderly driver in fatal crash released on police bail
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: An elderly man involved in a crash with a motorcycle that resulted in the rider's death yesterday has been released on police bail on health grounds. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the man in his 70s was detained to assist in investigations before being released on police bail. "Checks found that the elderly man has a valid driving licence.

items.title: MACC targets recovery of RM8b in remaining 1MDB assets, nearly 75pc already reclaimed
items.summary: “In all, I think there’s about US$2 billion (RM7.9 billion) still to be recovered,” Azam told a press conference here. The asset recovery efforts began in 2019, with RM1.155 billion retrieved that year, followed by RM14.257 billion in 2020 and RM5.117 billion in 2021. “Subsequently, RM400.38 million was recovered in 2022, RM8.088 billion in 2023, RM715.95 million in 2024 and RM1.566 billion in 2025,” Azam said.

items.title: South Korean judge who hiked ex-first lady's jail sentence found dead
items.summary: SEOUL: A South Korean judge who more than doubled the former first lady's prison sentence last month was found dead on Wednesday, police said. Shin Jong-o was "found unconscious around 1 am (1600 GMT on Tuesday)... at the Seoul High Court building", an investigator at Seocho district police station told AFP. Local media reported that Shin had left a suicide note, but the investigator said there was none.

items.title: Police arrest four suspects over fatal armed robbery in Teluk Intan
items.summary: TELUK INTAN: Police have arrested four suspects in connection with a gang armed robbery that resulted in the murder of an elderly man in Pekan Baru here on April 17. Hilir Perak district police chief Assistant Commissioner Chua Kok Lian said the suspects, aged between 20 and 30, were arrested on April 23 around Teluk Intan and Bentong, Pahang. "The other three suspects are involved in other robbery and cable theft cases that also occurred around Teluk Intan," he said at a press conference at the Hilir Perak district police headquarters here today.

items.title: US wants 'concrete actions' on Iran from next Iraqi PM
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States is looking for "concrete actions" by Iraq's next prime minister to distance the state from pro-Iran armed groups before resuming financial shipments and security aid, a senior official said Tuesday. But a senior US State Department official, speaking on condition of anonymity, said Zaidi must address the "blurry line" between pro-Iran armed groups in the Shia-majority country and the state. The official said US facilities in Iraq suffered more than 600 attacks after February 28, when the United States and Israel launched their war on Iran.

items.title: Welder gets 33 years jail, 32 strokes of rotan for raping stepdaughter
items.summary: Judge Zulkpli Abdullah handed down the sentence after the 40-year-old man pleaded guilty to all charges. For the first and second charges, the man was charged with raping his stepdaughter, who was 14 years and four months old at the time, in June 2022 and April 2023. These charges were framed under Section 376B(1) of the Penal Code, which carries a prison term of up to 30 years and whipping.

items.title: Education Ministry finalising new school safety guidelines
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Education Ministry is finalising new school safety guidelines aimed at strengthening protection measures in schools nationwide. In a statement today, the ministry said it is also consulting with experts, including the National Institute of Occupational Safety and Health Malaysia, particularly on improving protocols involving the use of vehicles within school compounds. "School administrators have also been reminded to consistently emphasise safe vehicle use within school premises to prevent further incidents," the statement read, The ministry also stressed that schools must remain a safe environment for students, teachers and the wider school community.

items.title: Padini probe: Eight held as MACC investigates frozen bank accounts
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained eight people to assist in investigations linked to the freezing of several bank accounts belonging to clothing retailer Padini Holdings Bhd and its subsidiaries. MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the investigation is ongoing, with statements already recorded from 10 people. It was reported that MACC had frozen certain bank accounts of Padini Holdings Bhd and its subsidiaries in connection with an ongoing investigation.

items.title: MACC detains eight in Padini-linked probe, statements recorded from 10
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 6 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained eight individuals to assist in investigations linked to the freezing of several bank accounts belonging to garment retailer Padini Holdings Bhd, and its subsidiaries. MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the probe is ongoing, with statements recorded from 10 individuals so far, while another 15 are being traced to assist investigations. The company also said it had engaged external legal counsel and taken necessary steps, including applying for relief to unfreeze the accounts.

items.title: Johor to form school safety task force after Year Two pupil killed by van in Batu Pahat
items.summary: Johor education and information committee chairman Aznan Tamin said the initiative was aimed at investigating and improving students’ safety inside and outside school premises. He said this also included school-based activities. On Nur Aini Umairah’s death, Aznan said the Johor Education Department is expected to provide counselling assistance to the victim’s family.

items.title: Deeper Malaysia–Philippines partnership crucial to shaping Asean’s future, says former Yang di-Pertua Negeri of Penang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Malaysia can play a vital role in shaping Asean’s future by working closely with the Philippines, the current Asean chair, to advance key regional initiatives, said former Yang di-Pertua Negeri of Penang Tun Ahmad Fuzi Abdul Razak. He said that among the proposed regional initiatives are the Digital Asean Commons, aimed at integrating digital economies, data governance and innovation ecosystems across the region, as well as the Regional Supply Chain Resilience Initiative, which aims to position Asean as the world’s most reliable and diversified manufacturing hub. “Other initiatives include Asean Green Transition Partnership, which aims to mobilise public and private sector investment in sustainable energy and climate resilience, and the Asean Talent Mobility Framework to facilitate the movement of skilled professionals across borders,” he said in his keynote address at the Asean Leadership and Partnership Forum (ALPF) 2026 here today.

items.title: Father remanded for alleged sexual assault against 12-year-old daughter
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A palm oil labourer has been remanded for seven days starting today for suspected physical sexual assault against his daughter since last year. Magistrate Nurasidah A Rahman issued the remand order against the 53-year-old suspect until Tuesday, enabling police to investigate the case under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017. The medical team noticed the student was depressed and withdrawn, and upon asking about her situation, the girl revealed that she had been a victim of sexual assault by her biological father since last year.

items.title: Rules on fully-imported EVs tightened, ministry raises entry bar for imports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry has introduced new requirements for fully imported (CBU) electric vehicles, effectively raising the threshold for market entry on both price and performance. From July 1, CBU EVs imported into Malaysia must carry a minimum cost, insurance and freight (CIF) value of RM200,000 and deliver at least 180 kW (around 241 hp). The new framework replaces the existing benchmark of a RM250,000 minimum selling price and 200 kW output requirement.

items.title: Pahang, Singapore deepen ties, explore investment and tourism cooperation
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail described the courtesy visit by the Singaporean delegation led by Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong as a meaningful platform for discussions to further strengthen bilateral ties. He said the meeting today provided a valuable platform to strengthen close relations between Pahang and Singapore, while paving the way to explore new areas of cooperation for mutual benefit. Wan Rosdy was accompanied by State Secretary Datuk Seri Nazri Abu Bakar, State Financial Officer Datuk Fadzilla Salleh, State Legal Adviser Datuk Muhammad Saifuddin Hashim Musaimi, Pahang Public Works, Transport and Health Committee chairman Datuk Ir Razali Kassim, and Pahang Investment, Industry, Science, Technology and Innovation Committee chairman Datuk Mohamad Nizar Najib.

items.title: Four months on the run, mechanic charged with murder of elderly man
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: After four months on the run, a mechanic was charged in the magistrate's court in Jawi here today with the murder of an elderly man last December. C. Jagadesh, 34, nodded in understanding after the charge was read to him before magistrate Nurul Ainna Ahmad. Jagadesh was charged with murdering Goh Aik Lai, 60, at BK Foodvillage, Taman Jelatik, Sungai Bakap, about 4pm on Dec 30, 2025.

items.title: PBAPP to install two main pipelines to boost water supply in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) will install two 1.8m diameter main pipelines to channel more treated water from the Sungai Dua Water Treatment Plant (WTP) to the Barat Daya district on the island as part of efforts to strengthen the water supply by 2030. PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the project is a key component of Phase 3 of the Water Contingency Plan 2030 (WCP 2030), with an estimated total cost of about RM416 million involving pipe procurement, installation works, excavation, road reinstatement works, and land acquisition costs. He said the first pipeline will connect the Sungai Dua WTP to the connection point of the Penang Third Undersea Twin Pipeline Project (3rd PTSP) in Butterworth, involving a route of about 13km on the mainland.

items.title: Palestinian ambassador hails Malaysia’s support, urges deeper role in post‑war rebuilding and trade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Palestine’s Ambassador to Malaysia Jehad Fouad Alqdra has praised Malaysia as one of Palestine’s most consistent and principled international supporters in its enduring struggle for statehood and self-determination. Palestine’s Ambassador to Malaysia Jehad Fouad Alqdra during an interview with Bernama at his office recently. The ambassador also invited Malaysia to participate in post-war reconstruction efforts in Gaza.

items.title: TH: Queue system and quota allocation remain sole criteria for haj eligibility
items.summary: MAKKAH: The queue system and quota allocation are the main criteria used by Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) to determine Malaysian pilgrims' eligibility, independent of financial capacity or higher package payments. Licensed haj operators (PJH) operations director Anwar Ahmad said all prospective pilgrims, including those applying through PJH packages, are subject to the same processes and criteria set by TH. "Ensure that every haj offer comes from TH and only deal with licensed haj operators to avoid the risk of fraud," he said.

items.title: PBAPP to lay two main pipelines under Penang water contingency plan to raise supply by 2030
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 6 — The Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) will install two 1.8-metre (m) diameter main pipelines to channel more treated water from the Sungai Dua Water Treatment Plant (WTP) to the Barat Daya district on the island as part of efforts to strengthen water supply by 2030. PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the project is a key component of Phase 3 of the Water Contingency Plan 2030 (WCP 2030), with an estimated total cost of about RM416 million involving pipe procurement, installation works, excavation and road reinstatement works, as well as land acquisition costs. He said the first pipeline will connect the Sungai Dua WTP to the connection point of the Penang Third Undersea Twin Pipeline Project (3rd PTSP) in Butterworth, involving a route of about 13 kilometres on the mainland.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng trial: No money trail did not weaken probe, court told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The absence of a money trail in Lim Guan Eng's corruption trial involving the Penang undersea tunnel project did not undermine the integrity of the investigation, the Sessions Court heard today. He said this when testifying as a prosecution witness in Lim's corruption trial involving the construction of a major road and undersea tunnel project in Penang worth RM6.3 billion. CZCSB was the company awarded the RM6.34 billion Penang undersea tunnel project.

items.title: India's cows offer biogas alternative to Mideast energy crunch
items.summary: ACROSS much of India, an energy crunch caused by the Iran war has prompted long queues for cooking gas cylinders. On a stove with blue flames, she flips a chapati flatbread, burning biogas produced from cow dung – an alternative fuel helping ease pressure on supplies. The biogas works for everything – "vegetables, tea, lentils", she said.

items.title: Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor urges public to learn from global tensions
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, has urged the public to draw lessons from ongoing global geopolitical tensions and their impact on economic and social stability. Also present was UiTM vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Shahrin Sahib@Sahibuddin. "UiTM must remain true to this mandate, and I do not consent to any attempt to alter or undermine its founding purpose," said Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, who is also the UiTM pro-chancellor.

items.title: MACC pursuing US$2b more in 1MDB asset recovery efforts
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is optimistic of recovering another US$2 billion (about RM7.9 billion) in assets linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal through ongoing international asset recovery efforts. MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said authorities are currently pursuing several major recoveries involving funds and assets held by individuals and entities connected to the scandal. "There are also several other ongoing recovery processes.

items.title: [UPDATED] Azam Baki: More govt officials may face MACC scrutiny
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has indicated that more government officials could come under scrutiny following recent investigations involving two director-generals of government agencies. MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the commission continuously monitored and verified information involving public officers before opening formal investigations. Azam also reiterated MACC's long-standing warning to civil servants against corruption and abuse of power.

items.title: Floods force 49 Raub villagers to evacuate homes
items.summary: RAUB: Villagers in Kampung Batu Talam here were caught off guard when continuous heavy rain in the early hours of today caused floods that inundated their homes. Water began entering the houses about 7am after Sungai Kapur Batu Talam in the settlement burst its banks, forcing families to evacuate to the SMA Al-Falah hall. "Heavy rain since 2am caused the river to overflow into the low-lying settlement, leaving homes submerged under up to 0.5 metres of water.

items.title: Malaysia’s 1MDB recovery hits RM31.3b as of 2025, ranks among world’s highest, says MACC chief
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 6 — Malaysia has successfully recovered RM31.3 billion, or 74.5 per cent, of the RM42 billion misappropriated from 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) funds as of last year, according to Tan Sri Azam Baki. Azam said that 1MDB asset recovery efforts began in 2019, with RM1.155 billion retrieved that year, followed by RM14.257 billion in 2020 and RM5.117 billion in 2021. “Subsequently, RM400.38 million was recovered in 2022, RM8.088 billion in 2023, RM715.95 million in 2024 and RM1.566 billion in 2025,” he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC proposes charging two over RM1.1bil semiconductor probe
items.summary: MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the investigation papers were nearing completion and are expected to be submitted to the deputy public prosecutor soon. He said investigators had recorded statements from 22 witnesses, including political analyst James Chai and former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. Azam disclosed that MACC investigators had identified two individuals whom they would recommend for prosecution.

items.title: Johor draws RM10b in potential investments after Shanghai trip, says exco
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 6 — Johor has attracted strong interest from 36 international investors, with potential initial investment commitments worth about RM10 billion, state investment, trade, consumer affairs and human resources committee chairman Lee Ting Han said today. Speaking during the question-and-answer session at the Johor state legislative assembly, Lee said the investment potential followed the Johor government delegation’s recent working visit to Shanghai, China. Lee said the delegation also held comprehensive discussions with several major industry players and their supplier networks to strengthen Johor’s investment potential and supply chain development.

items.title: Malaysian Pacific Industries poised for robust three-year growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI) is expected to deliver a robust three-year core net profit at a compound annual growth rate of 28 per cent supported by stronger contributions from the industrial segment. The company's US dollar revenue grew 31.6 per cent to US$465 million in the first nine months, driven by higher contributions across Asia, the US and Europe. Despite the headwinds, MPI's net profit rose 30.4 per cent YoY to RM144 million, supported by strong demand for power module packages driven by AI infrastructure rollouts, gradual recovery in the automotive segment and robust utilisation at Carsem Ipoh and Carsem Suzhou.

items.title: Man arrested after drug possession, police chase ends in three-car crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 31-year-old man found in possession of drugs was arrested after fleeing police and causing a three-car crash at a traffic light along Jalan Ampang near Gleneagles Hospital yesterday. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the suspect, who was driving a white Perodua Bezza, failed to stop when instructed by an Ampang police patrol unit, triggering a pursuit from Pandan Indah to the scene of the incident. "Wangsa Maju police have also opened investigations under Section 12(2) for possession of dangerous drugs and Section 15(1)(a) for self-administration of dangerous drugs under the Dangerous Drugs Act," he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] 1MDB recovery hits RM31.3bil, among world's highest in financial scandals
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recovered RM31.3 billion, or 74.5 per cent, of the RM42 billion misappropriated from 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB). MACC Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the marks one of the highest asset recovery rates globally for a large-scale financial crime scandal. He said the amount exceeded earlier projections of a 70 per cent recovery rate, which would have been about RM30 billion.

items.title: Google scientist says EU data measures pose privacy risk for users
items.summary: BRUSSELS: A top Google scientist sent a warning to EU antitrust regulators on Tuesday that its proposal requiring the company to share search engine data with rivals such as OpenAI risked exposing users' private information, the sternest rebuke yet in a tussle over Google's lucrative business model. The meeting comes a month after the Commission outlined a series of steps that Google should take to allow rival search engines access search data such as ranking, query, click and view data on fair, reasonable and non-discriminatory terms. The EU proposal, which will be finalised in the coming weeks following feedback from interested parties, has triggered a furious response from Google which called it regulatory overreach that could jeopardise users' privacy and security.

items.title: Police nab civil servant in Tawau break‑in case; recover laptops, bags
items.summary: District Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said the suspect was detained at about 10.45am by a team from the Criminal Investigation Department of the Tawau district police headquarters. Police said several items were reported missing, including two laptops, a 60-inch television, a wristwatch, several pairs of shoes, handbags and other personal belongings. Jasmin said police take house break-in cases seriously and urged the public to strengthen home security and report any suspicious activity immediately.

items.title: RT Pastry to issue 91.5mil new shares en route to Bursa listing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bakery specialist RT Pastry Holdings Bhd has signed an underwriting agreement with KAF Investment Bank Bhd for its initial public offering (IPO) on the Ace Market of Bursa Malaysia. RT Pastry specialises in the manufacturing and retail of premium pastry and bakery products, operating over 17 outlets in the Klang Valley. RT Pastry said 16.9 million of the new shares will be allocated for the public, 6.7 million shares for eligible persons of the company and 42.3 million shares for the private placement to Bumiputera investors approved by the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry.

items.title: Tabung Haji system ensures Haj eligibility based on quota and queue, not higher payments, says licensed operator
items.summary: MECCA, May 6 — The queue system and quota allocation are the main criteria used by Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) to determine Malaysian pilgrims’ eligibility, with no influence from financial capacity or higher package payments. Licensed Haj operators (PJH) operations director Anwar Ahmad said all prospective pilgrims, including those applying through PJH packages, are subject to the same processes and criteria set by TH. “Ensure that every Haj offer comes from TH and only deal with licensed Haj operators to avoid the risk of fraud,” he said.

items.title: Sessions Court allows removal of ‘received’ from charge in Lim Guan Eng tunnel graft trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng’s undersea tunnel corruption trial will continue with an amended charge, after the Sessions Court allowed an application by the prosecution today. Judge Azura Alwi said the amendment, which removes the word “received” from the first charge, would not prejudice the defence, according to the New Straits Times . Lim Guan Eng faces four corruption charges linked to the Penang undersea tunnel project, which was valued at about RM6.34 billion.

items.title: Anwar to lead Malaysian delegation to Cebu for 48th Asean Summit
items.summary: CEBU (Philippines), May 6 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will be leading the Malaysian delegation to the 48th Asean Summit and Related Meetings here, from May 7 to 8. “The 48th Asean Summit and Related Meetings will be an opportunity for Malaysia to share and exchange views on various issues on Asean Community building and the way forward. Meanwhile, on Thursday, Mohamad will attend the Asean Foreign Ministers’ Meeting, the 31st Asean Political-Security Community Council Meeting, the 38th Asean Coordinating Council Meeting, as well as the Asean Joint Foreign and Economic Ministers’ Meeting.

items.title: Police arrest man in Keningau over possession of homemade guns and air rifles without licence
items.summary: KENINGAU, May 6 — Police arrested a local man for allegedly possessing homemade firearms and air rifles without a licence during a raid at a house in Kampung Kuala Punteh, Sook, on Monday night. Acting Keningau District Police Chief DSP Alizam Dalaman said the arrest was made at about 10pm on Monday under Ops Laras by a team from the Criminal Investigation Department. Police found two homemade firearms known as bakakuk, two air rifles and 20 pellet rounds inside a bedroom of the house.

items.title: MACC eyes recovery of remaining 1MDB-linked artworks by year-end
items.summary: Outgoing MACC chief Tan Sri Azam Baki said the recovered assets would first be kept at MACC headquarters here, pending a decision by the Ministry of Finance on whether they should be retained or auctioned. “Overall, MACC has identified a total of 12 artworks to be repatriated to Malaysia,” he said. Among the artworks still in the process of being returned are paintings by Henri Matisse valued at US$52,500, Alexander Calder valued at US$125,000, Pablo Picasso valued at US$1.265 million, William H. Bailey valued at US$66,250, and Raoul Dufy valued at US$40,000.

items.title: Teen pleads not guilty to dangerous driving causing cyclist’s death in Kuching
items.summary: KUCHING, May 6 — A 19-year-old man claimed trial at the Magistrates’ Court here on Wednesday to a charge of dangerous driving causing death of a cyclist along Jalan Stutong last week. The accused pleaded not guilty before Magistrate Nursyaheeqa Nazwa Radzali after the charge under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 was read to him. According to the charge, the accused, who was travelling straight along the left lane, allegedly knocked into a cyclist in the same lane, causing the victim’s death.

items.title: King departs for Moscow at Putin’s invitation for Victory Day events
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has departed for Moscow at the invitation of President Vladimir Putin ahead of Russia’s Victory Day celebrations on May 9. Present at the Royal Hangar to bid farewell were Tunku Temenggong Johor Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Sultan Ibrahim, and Tunku Putera Johor Tunku Abu Bakar Al-Haj Sultan Ibrahim. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail, Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri, and Johor Police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad were in attendance.

items.title: Sarawak eyes UK partnership to develop satellites and semiconductor tech
items.summary: KUCHING, May 6 — Sarawak is exploring strategic partnerships in the United Kingdom to advance semiconductor design and satellite technology as part of its ambition to emerge as a high-value player in the global technology and aerospace sectors. Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Datuk Openg said the initiative, led by SMD Semiconductor, marks a significant step towards positioning the state as a high-value player within the global semiconductor, aerospace, and space technology ecosystem. He said this during the Sarawak Semiconductor engagement session jointly organised by SMD Semiconductor and the University of Bristol at the National Composites Centre in Bristol and Bath Science Park near Bristol, United Kingdom on Tuesday.

items.title: Sungai Muda water levels back to safe range after days of heavy rain
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 6 — Water levels in Sungai Muda are now at a safe level following heavy rainfall over the past few days, Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said. He said the river level is now above two metres, which is sufficient to meet current demand. He said PBAPP will continue to monitor the river water levels as it is the main source of raw water supply for Penang.

items.title: Two ‘sick’ school projects in Miri set for completion by June
items.summary: MIRI, May 6 — Construction of two school projects previously categorised as ‘sick projects’ – SK Sayed Othman and SMK Tudan – is expected to be fully completed by June this year. Miri MP Chiew Choon Man said SK Sayed Othman, which involves building and upgrading works, has reached 89 per cent completion, while SMK Tudan is now 99 per cent complete. Also present were Miri District Education Office (PPD Miri) officer Jumari Hashim and PPD Miri Planning Sector officer Awang Ali Awang Ahmad.

items.title: Usno suspends six senior leaders as internal party disputes deepen
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 6 — Six United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) members have been suspended with immediate effect amid an escalating internal party dispute. Party president Tan Sri Pandikar Amin Mulia said those suspended are deputy president I Datuk Ibrahim Linggam, Ahmad Kanajaan, Hussin Ibnu Hassan, Mohammad Hafeiz Hussin, Sazali Justi and Sallehudin Kasau. He said the action was taken under Clause 49.10 of the party constitution, which grants the president authority to suspend members deemed to be affecting party stability.

items.title: Teen couple in Melaka to be charged after alleged abortion attempt sends girl to hospital
items.summary: MELAKA, May 6 — Melaka police have reportedly uncovered two alleged concealment of birth cases this year, including one in which a teenage couple allegedly tried to terminate a five-month pregnancy using medication. According to the New Straits Times , Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the case involving a 17-year-old girl and her 18-year-old boyfriend came to light after a medical officer at Hospital Melaka lodged a report on April 25. Police investigations found that the couple had allegedly planned to terminate the pregnancy using pills.

items.title: Usno split deepens as info chief warns exit from GRS could weaken party
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 6 — United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) Information Chief Iskandar Zulkarnain Ramli has described the party’s decision to leave Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) as an unwise and hasty political move that could weaken its position. He said the move would be detrimental to Usno, particularly as the party currently holds no seats in the State Legislative Assembly. “As such, there is no reasonable justification for Usno to leave GRS based solely on personal political decisions,” he said.

items.title: Saudi envoy praises Malaysia ties, highlights smoother Haj services and reforms, says ‘daging dendeng’ a favourite
items.summary: In the interview, Osamah Dakhdel Al Ahmadi reflected on the steady progress of Malaysia-Saudi Arabia relations, underpinned by strong political will and expanding economic cooperation, particularly in line with Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030. He reiterated the Kingdom’s continued commitment to enhancing services for Haj pilgrims, including those from Malaysia, while outlining Saudi Arabia’s diplomatic approach to regional developments, emphasising stability, restraint, and dialogue. Economic cooperation between the Kingdom and Malaysia offers broad and growing opportunities, especially given the attractive environment for quality projects and value-added investments provided by the Kingdom’s Vision 2030.

items.title: Rafizi slated for third day at MACC over RM1.1b semiconductor investment probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli is expected to return to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) this afternoon to have his statement recorded for a third day. MACC Investigation Division senior director Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar said Rafizi arrived at the MACC headquarters at about 10am yesterday, according to report in Berita Harian . Mohd Hafaz reportedly said the second day of questioning ended at about 7pm, after nearly nine hours.

items.title: ‘No more excuse’: Guan Eng tells Anwar to reform Sosma in three months, match urgency on Mara Act
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — DAP veteran Lim Guan Eng today urged Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to amend the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 within three months, saying Putrajaya should show the same urgency it has given to proposed amendments to the Mara Act. In a statement, Lim said Anwar had opposed Sosma while in the Opposition, but has not shown the same urgency in reforming the law since becoming prime minister. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi later said the draft amendment could be brought to Cabinet within three weeks, ahead of Anwar’s deadline.

items.title: Horror crash on NSE near Gopeng: Perodua Axia driver dies after metal barrier tears through car
items.summary: Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations, Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad, said the department received an emergency call at 4.29 pm, after which a team from the Simpang Pulai Fire and Rescue Station was dispatched to the scene. “The accident involved a Perodua Axia and a Perodua Myvi. “The rescue team conducted an extrication operation using hydraulic equipment to remove the pinned victim.

items.title: Bukit Aman reshuffles 19 senior officers in major nationwide transfer exercise
items.summary: Abdul Rahman said Seberang Perai Tengah District Police Chief ACP Helmi Aris has been appointed as Head of JPJKK Penang JPJKK Chief, with the acting rank of SAC, while Bukit Aman Logistics and Technology Department, Facilities/Supply/Assets Division Chief Assistant Director (Police Supply Centre) ACP Amran Ab Rashid has been appointed Chief Assistant Director (Transport) of the Technical/Operations Division of the same department, with the acting rank of SAC. Abdul Rahman said Melaka Tengah District Police Chief ACP Christopher Patit has been appointed Johor Management Department Chief, with the acting rank of SAC, while North Klang District Police Chief ACP S.Vijaya Rao has been appointed Chief Assistant Director (Prevention Operations) at Bukit Aman JPJKK, with the acting rank of SAC. He said Hulu Selangor District Police Chief Supt Ibrahim Husin has been appointed Gombak District Police Chief while Selama District Police Chief Supt Sarudin Samah has been appointed Shah Alam District Police Chief, both with acting rank of ACP.

items.title: Police nab 39-year-old man in alleged cable theft along KTM line in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH, May 6 — Police have arrested a local man suspected of stealing cables belonging to Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) along the railway track at Kilometre 181.9 near Jalan Tun Perak Monday. Ipoh District Police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the arrest at 4.30 pm was carried out by KTMB auxiliary police together with personnel from the Ipoh District Police Headquarters (IPD) during a patrol. Police also seized a plastic bag containing three rolls of cable believed to belong to KTMB.
